headline.title: The AI skills gap is here, says AI company, and power users are pulling ahead
headline.summary: Anthropic finds AI isn’t replacing jobs yet, but early data shows growing inequality as experienced users gain an edge, raising concerns about future displacement and workforce divides.

simpleSummary: Today's edition was assembled directly from RSS feeds without AI curation.
Browse the stories below for the latest AI news.

tools.title: Google unveils TurboQuant, a new AI memory compression algorithm — and yes, the internet is calling it ‘Pied Piper’
tools.summary: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.
tools.content: Google’s TurboQuant has the internet joking about Pied Piper from HBO's "Silicon Valley." The compression algorithm promises to shrink AI’s “working memory” by up to 6x, but it’s still just a lab experiment for now.

tools.title: Bernie Sanders and AOC propose a ban on data center construction
tools.summary: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.
tools.content: Senator Bernie Sanders and Rep. Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez introduced companion legislation to halt construction on new data centers until Congress passes comprehensive AI regulation.

tools.title: Google launches Lyria 3 Pro music generation model
tools.summary: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.
tools.content: Google is launching Lyria 3 Pro, an upgraded music model that generates longer, more customizable tracks, as it expands AI music tools across Gemini, enterprise products, and other services.

creative.title: Google launches AI music generator Lyria 3 Pro, says it was trained on data it has the right to use
creative.summary: Google is making AI music longer and more widely available: Lyria 3 Pro generates songs up to three minutes long, complete with verses, choruses, and bridges. Google says the model was trained on content it has the right to use. That sets it apart fr
creative.content:
Google is making AI music longer and more widely available: Lyria 3 Pro generates songs up to three minutes long, complete with verses, choruses, and bridges. Google says the model was trained on content it has the right to use. That sets it apart from rival music generator Suno, which is facing legal battles with record labels over potential copyright infringement.

AI2 releases MolmoWeb, a fully open web agent that navigates websites using only screenshots. Despite having just 4 and 8 billion parameters, the models beat several larger proprietary systems on standard benchmarks.

creative.title: Arm breaks from its licensing-only model with first in-house chip built for AI data centers
creative.summary:
For the first time in its 35-year history, Arm has manufactured its own chip, expanding beyond its long-standing business model of licensing chip designs to companies like Apple and Nvidia.

research.title: Agentic commerce runs on truth and context
research.summary: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the p
research.content: Imagine telling a digital agent, “Use my points and book a family trip to Italy. Keep it within budget, pick hotels we’ve liked before, and handle the details.” Instead of returning a list of links, the agent assembles an itinerary and executes the purchase. That shift, from assistance to execution, is what makes agentic AI…

culture.content:
Lawmakers say moratorium on construction would buy time to create strong, federal guardrails for AI
Amid an unprecedented energy crisis and the rapid buildout of artificial intelligence infrastructure, progressive lawmakers have unveiled a new policy to place a moratorium on the construction of AI datacenters.

culture.title: Tell us: have you used AI to plan a holiday?
culture.summary:
We would like to hear from holidaymakers about their experiences of using AI
More people are turning to AI to design their holidays, whether that’s asking ChatGPT for inspiration or using AI trip planners. Almost a fifth of adults under 25 now use AI
culture.content:
We would like to hear from holidaymakers about their experiences of using AI
More people are turning to AI to design their holidays, whether that’s asking ChatGPT for inspiration or using AI trip planners. Almost a fifth of adults under 25 now use AI to plan breaks, according to a 2025 survey of 2,000 people by the UK travel industry body Abta. Overall, twice as many people said they used AI than the year before (8% in 2025 versus 4% in 2024). Over-55s were the least likely to use AI for holiday

culture.title: OpenClaw Agents Can Be Guilt-Tripped Into Self-Sabotage

culture.content: In a controlled experiment, OpenClaw agents proved prone to panic and vulnerable to manipulation. They even disabled their own functionality when gaslit by humans.
